summary: Completed testing of Capacitor Bearer Token Authentication feature. All 17 backend tests pass. Frontend compiles successfully (warnings only). UI tests confirm login/register pages work correctly with Google Auth buttons visible.
passed_tests: POST /api/auth/login returns access_token in JSON body
POST /api/auth/login returns refresh_token in JSON body
POST /api/auth/login returns user data (id, email, role)
GET /api/auth/me works with Bearer token (no cookies)
GET /api/auth/me returns 401 without token
GET /api/users/profile works with Bearer token
GET /api/users/impact works with Bearer token
GET /api/reports works with Bearer token
POST /api/auth/refresh with refresh_token in body works
POST /api/auth/refresh without token returns 401
New access token from refresh works on protected endpoints
POST /api/auth/register returns access_token and refresh_token
POST /api/auth/google endpoint exists (returns 401 for invalid session)
CORS preflight works for capacitor://localhost origin
Login works with capacitor://localhost origin
GET /api/notifications works with Bearer token
GET /api/users/subscription-status works with Bearer token
Frontend compiles without errors (only warnings)
UI: Login page loads with all elements (email, password, submit, Google button)
UI: Register page loads with all elements (username, email, password, Google button)
UI: Login flow works - redirects to home with success toast
critical_code_review_comments: tokenManager.js correctly implements dual-auth: localStorage persistence for native, memory-only for web
config.js exports HOSTED_WEB_URL for Google Auth redirect on native
AuthContext.js axios interceptor correctly: (1) attaches Bearer header on native, (2) sets withCredentials=false on native
LoginPage.js and RegisterPage.js correctly use HOSTED_WEB_URL for Google Auth redirect on native
Backend deps.py get_current_user() correctly accepts Bearer token from Authorization header (lines 134-153)
Backend /api/auth/login, /api/auth/register, /api/auth/google all return access_token and refresh_token in JSON body
Backend /api/auth/refresh accepts refresh_token from request body (not just cookies)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (compiles with warnings only, all UI elements verified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Capacitor Bearer Token Auth fully tested. The dual-auth architecture works: Web uses cookies (same-origin), Capacitor native uses Bearer tokens (cross-origin with withCredentials=false). The Emergent proxy returns access-control-allow-origin: * which is fine because native doesn't send cookies. Token persistence in localStorage survives app restarts. Google Auth redirect uses HOSTED_WEB_URL on native instead of capacitor://localhost.

verified_features.bearer_auth_on_impact: PASS - GET /api/users/impact works with Bearer token
verified_features.bearer_auth_on_reports: PASS - GET /api/reports works with Bearer token
verified_features.register_returns_tokens: PASS - access_token and refresh_token in JSON body
verified_features.axios_interceptor_bearer: PASS - AuthContext.js attaches Bearer header on native
verified_features.axios_interceptor_withCredentials: PASS - AuthContext.js sets withCredentials=false on native
verified_features.token_persistence_localStorage: PASS - tokenManager.js persists tokens in localStorage for native
verified_features.google_auth_redirect_uses_HOSTED_WEB_URL: PASS - LoginPage.js and RegisterPage.js use HOSTED_WEB_URL on native
